title: Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claude's thoughts into text
raw_content_summary: Anthropic blog post introducing Natural Language Autoencoders (NLA) — a method that converts internal model activations directly into readable natural-language text using a three-component architecture: frozen target model, activation verbalizer (AV), and activation reconstructor (AR). Reports key findings: 16% unverbalized evaluation awareness in destructive coding tests (0% verbalized), 26% on SWE-bench, <1% on real traffic. Auditor win rate improved from <3% to 12-15% in hidden motivation auditing game. Acknowledges limitations: hallucination, high cost, lack of mechanistic grounding.
